title: Dockerfile and Compose Validation, Image Tests, Fault Injection, and Quality Gates · Layer and Runtime Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective container release test and fault matrix; isolate the first causal fault in dockerfile and compose validation, image tests, fault injection, and quality gates and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in container release test and fault matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Run Dockerfile syntax and build checks, policy lint, warnings-as-errors, and negative fixtures without treating style as behavior proof.
Validate canonical Compose configuration, profiles, variables, images, networks, dependency conditions, implementation support, and changed overrides.
Test default user, entrypoint, arguments, files, permissions, labels, health, ports, signals, filesystem mutability, and exit behavior from the built image.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Build Checks Lint
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown. Competency probe: Run Dockerfile syntax and build checks, policy lint, warnings-as-errors, and negative fixtures without treating style as behavior proof.

Inspect the Docker Build Checks Lint trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Build Checks Lint.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for run dockerfile syntax and build checks, policy lint, warnings-as-errors, and negative fixtures without treating style as behavior proof. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Build Checks Lint, keep Docker Build Checks Lint connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Clean Host Transfer
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-clean-host-transfer-lnmzgk". Tailor its container-test-plan.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Clean Host Transfer.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Clean Host Transfer into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Clean Host Transfer, keep Docker Clean Host Transfer connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-clean-host-transfer-lnmzgk
  - competency: docker-clean-host-transfer
    artifact: container-test-plan.yaml
    decision: clean host transfer
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: move a tag while the immutable digest remains available
    retained-proof: per-platform manifest digest and selected image configuration
    case-key: lnmzgk
    change: |
      static: dockerfile-and-compose-validation
      image-contract: user-files-entrypoint-health
      integration: dependency-and-fault-injection
      transfer: clean-host-pull-and-run
      negative: readonly-slow-stop-missing-secret
    verify: docker buildx build --check . && docker compose config --quiet
    failure: Reject a successful build as runtime proof, a single happy-path curl, mutable local cache, or tests that depend on undeclared host state.
    repair: Layer structural, image, runtime, integration, fault, clean-host, and supply-chain checks with reproducible fixtures and teardown.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Docker Clean Host Transfer structural check is only one gate. container release test and fault matrix must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
